flat_text: Hi there, it's Dr. Smith from Babylon. Can you confirm your name, date of birth, and your home address, please? Um, it's 14 Foglelock, 9th Street, and it's in Bethesda. Can you tell her if we can hear her? Um, I can't hear you at the moment, actually. Could you turn up the volume up? Uh, can you give me the phone number? Yeah, that's better. Thank you. Um, okay. Sorry about that. Are you in a private place so you can have a consultation? Um, yes. What can I do for you today? Um, I've been having this blinding headache for a few days, and it's, like, really bothering me. I find it very hard to focus, or, like, I can't even go to work. Okay. And any headaches in the past before this? Not really, just day on and off. I recently had one for, like, 2 days, but it just went away with ibuprofen, so nothing like for longer than 2 days before. So how did this start? Um, it just started all of a sudden. I woke up one morning with a headache, and that was 3 days ago, and I just keep having it. It doesn't really go away with ibuprofen either. I tried that, but it just— it gets a little bit better, but, like, it doesn't go away. Okay. And whereabouts in your head is the pain? Um, it's kind of all over, but I feel like over my right eye it's a bit stronger. Okay. Any problems with your vision, or any problems tolerating the light? Um, light's definitely making me more sensitive. Like, I don't really like strong light. And, yeah, vision, I think. I get this shooting light sometimes. I'm not sure how to explain it. Just a bit of a weird kind of vision. Okay. And any nausea or vomiting? Um, no. I'm feeling a bit sick, but no vomiting. Any tingling or numbness or weakness anywhere? Um, no. Any change in your smell or taste? No, not at all. And in the past, when you've had headaches, what have they been like? Um, I would say quite normal headaches, just a bit of a pressure in between my eyes, but nothing major. Okay. And with this, has it been that all the time, or is it coming and going? Kind of all the time. So when I take ibuprofen, it gets a bit better, so it's a bit, like, less— it bothers me less, but then I still have it. It's still there. And what would you say the severity is? If 10 is the worst pain you've ever had, and 0 is nothing, what would you say the pain is like? I guess it's mostly 8, and then it kind of comes down to maybe a 5. Okay. And what about at nighttime? Are you able to fall asleep? Um, yeah. I'm sleeping well. Is it waking you up in the nighttime? No, no, no. But I can wake up with it in the morning. Okay. Any worse when you change position, or bend over, or cough, or anything like that? Um, sometimes when I stand up quickly, I get a bit of a stabbing pain. Or if I turn my head, like, I kind of feel like something is moving inside. Okay. And have you had any eye checks recently? Um, yeah. I've actually had one recently, and nothing changed in my vision. Okay. And have you had any cold symptoms, sore throat, sore ears, or pain over the sinuses? Any cough or phlegm? No, no. Any nosebleeds, or cough you up any blood, or anything? No. And just recently, have you been well, or have you had any general problems, any change in your weight, or tiredness, lack of energy, anything like that? Um, no change in weight. Maybe lack of energy, but maybe because I'm just having this headache, so I find it quite difficult to go through my daily activities. But, you know, when you have this constant headache. Have you been at work recently? Um, I work from home, but I couldn't really manage to do it all the time, so sometimes I just do the day off. So you've been— you've had a headache for 3 days. How many days have you missed off work? Um, so I worked from home all these 3 days, and the first day I worked from home, and then the second day I just took the whole day off. And today I just tried to work from home the first half of the day, but I just stopped in the morning because I couldn't focus. Okay. And any other medical problems in the past? Um, I think I only had abdominal pain when I was younger, but no cause was found, and that went away. But that's the only thing I know of. Okay. Have you been abroad recently at all? No. Do you take any medications? Um, just a pill. Which one? Uh, Myrcellin. And when did you start that? Um, about a year ago. Any side effects with that? Uh, no, I don't think so. Have you noticed any increased frequency of headaches since you started that? Um, no. Because this headache's just very recent, so nothing. About 3 years ago, but nothing really changed. Okay. And when you've had— when you described your headaches in the past, you said they were just general. Did you have any visual symptoms, flashing lights? No. Nausea, vomiting with them? No, nothing. None of that. Have you had your blood pressure checked recently with your pill? Um, yeah. I had it checked in the pharmacy when I got the pill, and they said it was normal. And when was that? About a year ago. Okay. And any allergies to anything? No. And who do you live with at home? Sorry? Who do you live with at home? Uh, just by myself. Okay. And do you smoke? Uh, no. Do you drink any alcohol regularly? Yeah. How much in a normal week, do you think? Um, probably, like, 3 beers once a week. Okay. And do you take any other drugs at all? Um, no, apart from the ibuprofen that I take sometimes when the headache gets really bad. Nothing else. Okay. So, I mean, it's obviously the first time you've had such a bad headache, and you said you'd had some altered light in front of your eyes as well? Yeah. But you haven't had a high temperature? Have you checked your temperature? Uh, no, I didn't. But I don't really feel feverish or anything. Okay. I mean, it could possibly be a migraine-type headache, but the fact it's all ongoing for 3 days, we probably should book you into an appointment to get an examination. Obviously, if it settles in the meantime, then we can cancel that, but we should do a neurological examination and look at the back of your eyes as well. Okay. In terms of medication, I can prescribe you something similar to ibuprofen, but a bit stronger, which you can take instead of ibuprofen. And you can also take paracetamol at the same time. Okay. So if you're happy to book an appointment with the support line for us to do a full examination and start the medication which I've prescribed you to take it with food. If you're— you said that when you go to bed, your headache's okay and not waking you up in the nighttime. Yeah, it's not. But you mentioned migraine, and that reminded me that my mom suffers from— like, she has migraines. Okay. Any other family history of medical problems? Um, no, I think that's it. But I know that my mom, like, she has quite bad migraines. She, like, has the vomiting and everything like that. Okay. That's fine. I mean, if in the meantime, before your appointment, your headache is getting unbearable, getting worse, or changing, then if it's that bad, then you should go to A&E and have an examination. Otherwise, you can call us for further advice on that. Mm-hmm. Okay. When you have an examination with us, we'll check your blood pressure as well, just to make sure your pill is still appropriate for you, because sometimes if we do think it's a migraine, then you would have to change your pill if you've had these flashing lights and things like that. Is there anything in the pill that can cause it, or is it? Well, some— so that pill, if you were having regular migraines with flashing lights or other symptoms, it wouldn't be suitable to stay on that pill, particularly. We'd have to change it to something else. All right. But you can carry on with that for the moment. Okay. Have you got any other questions? Um, so would you, like, send me a referral to a specialist, or do I have to be in a board to book an appointment? Yeah, if you book an appointment with the support team, then we can do a full examination then. Okay. Perfect. All right, then. Take care. Thank you very much. Bye.
